task: V3.15 Task 3/5 — Relation + Realism Gold Protocol Upgrade
main_remaining_risk: v2 protocol is DRAFT and has not been piloted. Annotators have not been trained on Layer 2 (realism). The 9-class realism issue taxonomy may have low inter-annotator agreement (heuristic rules from the automatic audit may not align with human judgment). The pre-registered 25% class-level and 25% paper-level realism downgrade thresholds are untested — if realism agreement is poor (Pearson r < 0.40), the realism layer becomes exploratory and the downgrade triggers cannot fire. Additionally, the 50-row pilot is small; wide confidence intervals mean borderline cases (e.g., 24% vs 26% high-realism rate) should not trigger mechanical decisions without qualitative review.
recommended_next_action: 1) Identify two independent annotators + one adjudicator (same as v1 plan, but train on v2 guideline including Layer 2). 2) Distribute v2 A/B templates + v2 guideline + v2 decision tree + v2 agreement plan + v2 adjudication protocol. 3) Annotators work independently (~4 hours each, including Layer 2). 4) Adjudicator performs two-layer adjudication per v2 protocol. 5) Compute v2 agreement metrics (Layer 1 + Layer 2). 6) Apply pre-registered decision rules (taxonomy revision, paper claim downgrade, realism downgrade). 7) Cross-check automatic realism audit (v3_15_claim_realism_audit) against gold realism scores to calibrate heuristic rules. 8) Decide: proceed to full 444-pair gold annotation, revise taxonomy, or downgrade paper claim.
